UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user interacting with the LINE Expense Bot, storing non-authentication related information and linking to LINE's unique user ID.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

lineUserId.description: The unique identifier provided by the LINE messaging platform for this user.

displayName.description: The user's display name as provided by LINE, for display purposes. This field is optional and may not always be available.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines categories for expenses, which can be system-defined or user-created for better organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the category (e.g., 'Food', 'Transport', 'Utilities').

description.description: A detailed description or example usage for the category.

isSystemDefined.description: Indicates whether this category is a predefined system category (true) or a custom user-created one (false).

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this category, if it's not system-defined. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Category)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Stores individual expense records made by users, including details like amount, description, date, and categorization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who recorded this expense. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

amount.description: The monetary value of the expense.

currency.description: The currency code for the expense (e.g., 'USD', 'BRL', 'JPY').

description.description: A brief description of the expense, usually parsed from the user's message or provided manually.

date.description: The specific date when the expense occurred.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category entity assigned to this expense. (Relationship: Category 1:N Expense)

originalMessage.description: The full, original LINE message from which the expense details were extracted, for auditing or re-processing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was first created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores non-authentication related user profile information. The `userProfileId` matches the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). Authorization relies on `userProfileId` matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores custom categories defined by a specific user. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence and `isSystemDefined: false`. Authorization relies on the path's `userId` matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this category, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user's custom category.

definition.description: Stores system-defined categories available to all users. These categories have `isSystemDefined: true`. Read access is typically granted to all authenticated users, while write access is restricted to backend services or admin roles.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the system-defined category.

definition.description: Stores individual expense records for a specific user. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence. Authorization relies on the path's `userId` matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who recorded this expense, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense record.

firestore.reasoning: The proposed Firestore data structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient queries.User profiles are stored in a top-level collection `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}` where `userProfileId` is the Firebase `request.auth.uid`. This makes user profile data directly accessible and secured based on the authenticated user's ID.User-specific entities like `Expense` and user-defined `Category` are nested under a user's unique path: `/users/{userId}/expenses/{expenseId}` and `/users/{userId}/categories/{categoryId}` respectively. This hierarchical structure inherently links these documents to their owner, making authorization rules straightforward (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`). This fulfills the 'Private Data' and 'Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data' access modeling mandates.For system-defined categories, a separate top-level collection `/categories_system/{categoryId}` is created. This collection adheres to the Structural Segregation principle by separating globally accessible read-only data (for users to select from) from user-specific data. Rules for this collection would typically allow `read` for all authenticated users, while `write` operations would be restricted to backend services or specific admin roles (if implemented).Authorization Independence is achieved by mandating that `userId` (representing the owner) is a field directly within `Expense` and user-defined `Category` documents. This denormalization means that security rules evaluating access to an expense or a category do not need to perform a `get()` operation on a parent `UserProfile` or `/users/{userId}` document. The `userId` embedded in the document, matching the `userId` in the path, directly provides the authorization context. This eliminates the complexities associated with `get()` calls in rules, enhancing transaction atomicity and debugging ease.Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs) are supported by this design. For example, to retrieve all expenses for a user, a query like `db.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('expenses').get()` implicitly filters by the authenticated user's ID, and the security rules ensure that only data belonging to `request.auth.uid` can be read from that path. Similarly, listing user-defined categories or reading system categories is inherently secure and efficient due to the clear structural segregation and embedded authorization fields.
